summary: reconcileStreetsAction per-row position writes transiently collide with non-deferrable UNIQUE (route_id, position); any non-append edit → PSQLException 23505 → PUT 500s.
failure_scenario: Confirmed empirically: the PR's own RouteBuilderControllerSpec test 'replace the street list in place' FAILS with 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint route_street_route_id_position_key Key (route_id, position)=(15, 0) already exists'.

summary: Mission gained userRouteId field; this pre-existing test still constructs Mission with 16 args → whole test suite fails to compile.
failure_scenario: sbt testOnly → '[error] ExploreAddressServiceSpec.scala:544:32: not enough arguments ... Unspecified value parameter userRouteId'; no test can execute until None is appended.

summary: insertIfNew resolves the route_street row by joining on street_edge_id and taking .head, so for a route containing the same street twice (out-and-back, explicitly enabled by 344.sql dropping the (route_id, street_edge_id) unique key) every audit links to the first row; the second-traversal row is never linked, updateCompleteness never marks the user_route complete, and getRouteTask re-serves the same street forever.
failure_scenario: Route [..., A reverse=false, A reverse=true]; explore it: first A audit links row1; on resume getRouteTask finds row2 unlinked and issues street A again, insertIfNew's .head again picks row1, row2 stays unlinked — completed never true, server keeps sending the user back to street A; client TaskContainer.nextTask (322) filters by streetEdgeId, compounding by silently skipping the return-leg task.

summary: saveRoute accepts an empty streets array (only updateRoute validates); route-scoped mission branch returns None at distance 0, so opening the route in Explore throws NoSuchElementException.
failure_scenario: POST /saveRoute with streets [] → 200 + route_id; GET /explore?routeId=<id> → ExploreService.scala:250 .map(_.get) throws → 500; route invisible in /userapi/routes (inner join) so owner can't delete from dashboard.
